capabilities: Systematically identify security vulnerabilities in a codebase using a defined scanning procedure.

procedure.action: Load the codebase into a static analysis tool such as SonarQube or OWASP Dependency-Check by specifying the root directory and ensuring all dependencies are included using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.
Execute predefined security scan rules by configuring the tool to use the latest security rule set updates; ensure rules cover OWASP Top Ten vulnerabilities.

Execute the scan process and capture the output by running the tool's CLI command; redirect output to a structured log file for further analysis using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Render a detailed report by parsing the structured log file; include severity levels, file paths, and line numbers for each identified vulnerability using deterministic template rendering with seeded RNG; assert output matches expected schema before persisting; assert generation_error_count < 1 and output_size_bytes > 0.

Inspect the generated report using a security expert to prioritize vulnerabilities based on severity and potential impact; document remediation steps by applying the checklist line by line; assert mandatory_item_fail_count < 1; log review_duration_ms and reviewer_id to review_log.

summary: Procedure for scanning codebases to identify security vulnerabilities using predefined rules and automated tools.
title: Security Vulnerability Identification in Codebases

failureConditions: False negatives when scan rules are outdated, causing critical vulnerabilities to remain undetected and exploited by attackers.
False positives when scan rules are too broad, causing unnecessary remediation work and resource wastage.
Incomplete scans when dependencies are not fully loaded, causing missed vulnerabilities in third-party libraries.
metrics: output matches expected schema before persisting
all vulnerabilities have severity and location details
successConditions: scan_duration < 30 minutes for codebases < 100k LOC; ensure timely feedback.
false_positive_rate < 5%; validate by manual review of a sample of reported vulnerabilities.
